message:
fix(budget): use min rather than saturating_add for min-plus convolution tail rate

For two min-plus curves f and g with tail rates r_f and r_g, the
convolution (f ⊗ g)(t) = inf_s [f(s) + g(t-s)] has asymptotic growth
rate min(r_f, r_g), not r_f + r_g. The previous implementation used
saturating_add, which overestimated the tail rate and caused
beyond-horizon extrapolation to grow faster than the true convolution.

This is a direct consequence of the infimal convolution definition:
as t → ∞, the minimizing split s* concentrates on the slower curve,
so the growth rate is dominated by the slower (minimum) rate.

Adds a Lemma 15 regression test that constructs curves with rates 1
and 3, verifies the convolution tail rate is min(1,3) = 1, and
cross-checks beyond-horizon extrapolation against a brute-force
evaluation at t=20.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
